qa_pairs.question: How can a user prevent an Ansys Workbench batch job from overwriting the solution file, which is useful for scaling tests on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To prevent an Ansys Workbench batch job from overwriting the solution file upon successful completion, change `Save(Overwrite=True)` to `Save(Overwrite=False)` in the last line of your Slurm script. This modification is beneficial for scaling tests, as it allows you to easily evaluate how well the simulation performs when increasing the number of cores without having the initialized solution overwritten by each test job.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for generating an input file for an Ansys Mechanical (APDL) job on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: An Ansys Mechanical (APDL) input file can be generated from within an interactive Workbench Mechanical session. To do this, click on `Solution -> Tools -> Write Input Files`, then specify a `File name:` like `YOURAPDLFILE.inp` and choose `APDL Input Files (*.inp)` as the `Save as type:`. Once created, these APDL jobs can then be submitted to the queue using the `sbatch script-name.sh` command.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key Slurm script settings and commands required to run a Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) Ansys Mechanical (APDL) job on an Alliance cluster's CPU nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: For a Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) Ansys Mechanical (APDL) job on an Alliance cluster's CPU nodes, the Slurm script should specify `--account` (your project account), `--time` (job time limit), `--mem` (total memory for all cores), `--nodes=1` (as SMP typically runs on a single node), and `--tasks` (the number of cores). After loading the `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03` modules (or newer), the job is executed using the `mapdl` command with parameters such as `-smp`, `-b nolist`, `-np $SLURM_NTASKS`, `-dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID`, and `-i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`. On the Cedar cluster, you may also need to set `export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$EBROOTGCC/../lib/gcc`.
